props.content: The Cosmogony era is when the universe of Grand Tale begins. Save for fourth wall breaks, nothing canonically exists prior to this point. Glooey placed an egg, which hatched into the Taleverse.

The Grand Era is marked by the founding of the Grand Tale, and it
emcompasses all current events.
This handbook is publicly available at
https://rimelight.com/careers/rimelight-guidebook
. For Rimelight employees it's also available in our internal tools, where they help us maintain and improve it.
All employees are mandated to read this document during our onboarding process, and are expected to act according to the norms listed within.
Rimelight Entertainment reserves the right to change any rules herein at will and without notice.
This handbook does not constitute a legally binding contract.

We have various other guidelines and similar documents for more specific areas within the company. They may be found within their respective parts of our internal documentation, but some are also
publicly available at _.

Hello! My name is Daniel Marchi, and I am the creator of Rimelight Entertainment. While the entirety of this handbook has been created through the contribution of multiple people, this first section has been singlehandedly written by me, undergoing only a legal proofreading to ensure non-lawyer me doesn't create any unwanted issues. No employee has authorization to alter this section in any way, _. Whilst this document is publicly available for anybody to read, if you're reading it there's a high chance today is your first day of work at Rimelight Entertainment - congratulations on the position! Therefore, I'm now personally delivering your first two assignments: 1. Read this handbook in its entirety at least once. 2. Engage with our products - play games, watch videos, read comics, you choose! That's it! Your first day here is entirely dedicated to these two activities.

we can't represent the opinion of every employee, we're not a
political party and nobody voted anybody in we have general
ideologies, by working here or using our products you're by
extension being tolerant of such views. if you don't like that you
are always free to disassociate with our brand. What we understand
you to want and try to provide you with as much as possible: -
Income. - A physically and psychologically safe work environment. -
Inexistant negative impact in your life outside of working hours. -
Opportunities to do meaningful things. create an environment where
players and developers can create and share meaningful experiences.
Throughout this document and the rest of our website, direct commentary from me is contained within Callouts like this one.

The name "Rimelight" is composed of a portmanteau between “rime” and “light”, concepts present in our intellectual properties. They represent the existential struggle between nihilist and existentialist philosophy, and how we live the story of our lives.

Our customers should have a general knowledge of where their money
goes, how our time gets spent and how decisions are made. -
Publicly available financial reports. - Publicly available
internal documents and guidelines. - Publicly available
development roadmaps and issue trackers. salary ranges,
compensation policies

- Uncompromising penalties for cheats, exploits, harassment, or
any other form of rule breaking from any member of our community
and staff. - _ - Sponsorship and partnership contracts with
companies and creators who align with our values.Security &
Privacy Using any of our products involves trusting us with your
time, money, and information, therefore these should be handled
with care and respect. - Regular third-party audits and rigid
incentives towards regulatory compliance. - Clear descriptions
provided on what data we collect and how it gets used, as well as
where it might end up. - Tools for autonomous and parental control
as well as guidance material to promote healthy and secure usage
of our products.first-party is best party *What about money? Isn't
this a large capitalist corporation?* Yes, it is - and it
definitely requires money to keep running. However, generating
revenue has never been a focus and never will be. It is an
important part of the system and certainly a key point involved in
decision making, but _. We are more than confident in the ability
of our products generating enough revenue to sustain themselves
without exploitative efforts toward that. , and keeping our
company independent is a very important _ for that. Rimelight
Entertainment is completely owned by me and has very loose
financial ties to our publisher _. What truly matters in here is
what matters to me, and buying yachts and private jets is not in
my bucket list.

Careers - Analytics & Research - Art, Animation, & Sound - Corporate
Operations - Community, Marketing, & Communications - Game Design -
Engineering & Technology - ESports, Events, & Broadcast Media -
Production & Project Management - Quality Assurance, Localization, &
Customer Service - User Experience & Visual Design

A collection of useful things to know about Rimelight Entertainment.
- I am the sole legal owner of Rimelight Entertainment. - Rimelight
Entertainment is the legal owner of all intellectual property that
it uses. We aim to achieve the highest salaries in the industry, and
for compensation bonuses to scale relative to the company's
performance.

Unlike many games, Grand Tale does not utilise the concepts of "dropping" or "equipping" items.
Other than floating point code limitations, items have no stack limit.

https://github.com/StudioSyndiCatCaius/OmegaGameFramework ‍ Overview The
Rimelight Game Framework is an Unreal Engine Plugin designed to contain
major core functionality for the development of games. It follows a
series of internal design patterns and methodologies, and is highly
modular Although primarily developed for internal use within the company
it’s built as highly generic and modular, allowing for external
distribution through a public Git repository. Modules RGF is divided
into two Modules, an editor and a runtime module.
RimelightFrameworkEditor , and is not packaged with the game builds.
RimelightFrameworkRuntime , and is packaged with game builds. ‍ Warning
All Framework classes are declared as Abstract, and are meant to be
subclassed by each game. RimelightFrameworkEditor Asset Import & Export
Data Validation RGF utilizes the Data Validation plugin to ensure assets
have been properly configured once imported. RimelightFrameworkRuntime
Saving & Loading Settings Keybinds Enhanced Input System Input

Behind the Scenes is a monthly livestream meant to show the making of
content, may include art, sound, design, concept, gameplay, balancing,
etc. Doesnt have to be of a single area, a single livestream might cover
various topics. Usually with a showcase of images and videos, as well as
live playthroughs narrated by a developer.Naming: Game Name: Behind the
Scenes - Content Name Release Schedule: Monthly on the _ week.Duration:
30 to 60 minutesCinematicsEvent CoverageLivestreamsData Streams, Lore
talksLets PlayQ&APodcasts?Official Soundtrack

Patch Highlights are meant as a quick overview of a patchs key points as
well as a brief explanation of the reasoning behind certain
changes.Naming: Game Name: Patch Highlights - Patch Number Release
Schedule: Weekly on a patch release (Monday?).Duration: 2 to 5
minutesPodcastsTrailers

Secret society style organization, few but very powerful members. perhaps
they have minions not technically evil, more like a group of
anti-heroes/anti-villains sort of agree to disagree a magneto vs xavier
conflict given these artifacts are difficult to obtain, they use them for
the time being to help in gathering the rest. only when theyre all
gathered together will they be destroyed. its also not easy to destroy
them Their objective is collecting all the Elder Artifacts - to prevent
the enemies from getting them while such immense power exists, the
universe will never be free think beyond good and evil Ultimately both
groups have the same goal, to get the artifacts out of the picture, but
the Kinship wants them protected, while the Kindred wants them destroyed.
One group argues its a matter of proper use, while the other believes
they're better off without them existing at all. Perhaps the kinship sees
theres more to them than just being tools that theyre there for a purpose,
to be learned from, which is why they host the convergence
and is considered a sovereign state.
Operating the kingdom and estate, cleaning, maintenance, food, construction, etc
printing and distributing the ccg

Resolves quests around the world and operations which require
specialized personnel. Strongest members in the Kinship, aside from the
leaders.
Resolving large scale conflicts.

Mana came directly from Glooey. It created lots of it. Then used it to
create the Tools. They then created everything. Glooey added a little
"oomph" in the mix, to destabilize symmetry and make interesting things
happen.There is nothing mana can't do.maximum manamana regenerationmana
costpresent in every living creaturealso present in
objectsMagicaptitudehas to be
learnedhttps://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/MusclesAreMeaningless

Mana is like dark energy/the forceit permeates the entire universe, and
can be concentrated within objects, living beings, locations, etcwhile
it is generally "everywhere", it is not uniformly distributed. the
"main" planet of our story happens to be a place with particularly high
concentration.Magic is the "burning"/manipulation (should it be
consumed?) of mana to release energy, which can be used for various
processes.Upon being consumed through the use of magic, beings and
objects can slowly replenish their depleted reserves.all mana in the
universe was created during the emergence, and in time will inevitably
be depleted as it gets used as magical fuel.Living beings can accumulate
stores of mana inside them, serving as a stockpile for later use. Once
consumed, these stores can slowly replenish by the passive absorption of
mana from the environment, much like osmosis. The total amount a being
can store, as well as how fast they can replenish their reserves can be
improved with training.how much mana a being has stored usually
corresponds to how powerful they areTypes of MagicRunesSpellsthe actual
abilities characters and creatures use, usually
namedEnchantments"Chakrasmithing"/forgingAgingImbuingSealing (could also
seal creatures like priston crystals, or naruto summons etc,
spirits)blood/lifeforceCrystalsmana concentrates on them, and their
crystallized formations can be used to channel and amplify magic through
themelderelder magic is the only strong enough to actually create or
destroy things. everything else merely manipulates it. it is found only
on glooey and on the elder tools
While each branch is associated to a specific set of abilities, it is possible to create spells which employ aspects of more than a single branch of magic.
auras?bonds/links, connection magicInfusion Voodoo idols totems

Zev was designed as a representative of the Romani people.

  A snake was chosen as Zev’s pet due to it’s symbolic relationship to
  Grand Tale’s theme of life, such as the Serpent of Immortality and the
  Ouroboros.
  The Kris was chosen as Zev’s weapon due to its and shape aligning with
  his visual language, as well as the existence of the Romani court by
  the same name.

Born in the city of _, Vale was the son of an esteemed scholar
verging a breakthrough in time science through the construction of
the Eden Rings, magical contraptions connecting the present to the
past. Although close at childhood, Vale grew distant from his father
and resented his studies, viewing them as pointless efforts for
nothing but a dream - reuniting with Eden, Vale’s mother. During one
of his father’s experiments a catastrophic accident snapped one of
the rings, absorbing him into a time rift and leaving no trace
behind. Blaming his own absence for the fate of his father, Vale
spent the next years of his life studying what was left of the rings
and perfecting them, seeking to locate and rescue his father from
the valleys between time itself.
Vale's thematic is based on the concept of the
Arrow of Time
The name "Vale" loosely means "of the valley".

props.content:
Overview --- - Creator of every universe. - It is aware it is fictional.
- Every universe is a franchise. - There is no multiverse other than the
ones explicitly created by Glooey. Glooey is the primordial Lightsprite,
creator of the Grand Tale universe and avatar of The Light. # Appearance
--- Glooey is made entirely of The Light and has no need for a physical
body, but occasionally chooses to visibly manifest itself to interact with
other beings, doing so in two different forms. In its Primeval Form it
possesses a _ visage. Its body is ethereal energy with a royal blue tint .
It possesses three sets of wings. In its Corporeal Form it is very similar
to its Spiritual Form, but with much more subtle features. # Personality
--- Glooey is usually uninterested in directly intervening in any of the
universe's affairs. It acts mostly as an observer, but is open to being
interacted with should any find the means to. It often manifests itself at
important moments, but only shows itself in its Primeval Form for
exceptionally significant events. # Biography --- Glooey was birthed from
the Light itself in the beginning of all things. Shortly after its
creation it spawned the universe and the six Elder Artifacts, spreading
them across the cosmos to create an environment within which stories could
unfold. # Relationships --- # Abilities --- Glooey is both omnipotent and
omniscient, as well as immortal. It is also able to break the 4th Wall and
directly reference the user. # Trivia --- - Glooey's Corporeal Form is the
official Rimelight Entertainment mascot, being often used in branding and
even as an information vector for tutorials and the like. - Glooey's
visual design was inspired by the Glaucus atlanticus, a blue sea slug
native to temperate and tropical waters around the globe. ## Etymology ---

His main thematic colours are red (see Red Is Heroic), white, and
gold.He has a light skin tone and Y

reckles.He is of average

Aleph has a bright and cheerful personality.Aleph is a vegetarian.He
is written to represent a gifted individual, with particular
emphasis on the concept of overexcitabilities.

While Aleph is certainly clever, that is never to be the focus in
any narrative involving him.
Aleph is adept at Augmentation, Conjuration, and Incantation.

nation

Cooperative Campaign? 3 v 3 Competitive? 3 Player Cooperative? Elements of
4X for meta progression? Grand Strategy map pvp battles are training
routines, powered by the cradle? building a massive base with multiple
expansions, microing fast upgraded armies, setting up an empire morality,
moral struggles of the cast, diplomacy, culture, setting differences aside
large scale conflict figure out what the fun parts really are and focus on
expanding and evolving them - cut out the rest incentives to move out on
the map, explore, set forward positions possible "large minimap" hotkey
like an arpg, maybe enlarge it in place while holding alt 2v2 being the
"standard" game mode? integrate more cooperation different balancing for
each mode, 1v1 and 2v2 etc? lower early game lethality, feeling of phases
like a moba game? defender's advantage Second, we’re going to look at
lethality in order to enable endlessly scalable difficulties. SC2 Co-op
was built around deleting waves instantly either via your units’ high DPS
or very powerful “nuke”-style calldowns. By designing our game with less
lethal units, we open ourselves up to greater opportunities for micro
within a given fight and greater opportunities to debuff, which is
generally very effective on stronger enemies. Less focus on calldowns that
strictly do damage. They Are Billions takes this concept to another level
where every building has unique abilities/adjacency bonuses, and
base-building is almost half the game. That being said, we recognize that
They Are Billions is a single-player RTS and thus the player can be
expected to pay a greater % of his attention to this particular aspect of
the game. combined supply and production? design space for additional
factions? make the narrative and design in a way to not limit that option,
even if we choose not to do it. high expansion incentives, and multiple
points of interest on maps to split forces War Council main base grants
double as many resources as expansions, lower percentile increase in
income when you expand Resources third resource, like immortal's pyre?
used for hero abilities etc, similar to sc2 campaign's spear of adun

and are the primary resource for building structures, training units and
researching upgrades. Anima Anima is gathered from Anima Strands and are
the secondary resource for building structures, training units and
researching upgrades. Population Cap Population is a and has a maximum cap
of 200. can be increased by certain structures and dictates the maximum
amount of units that can be owned. maximum 200 town hall provides 20 cap,
workers have 1 size, Sending resources, sharing resources Resources are

by Air attacks) Damage Types Physical Damage Tooltip: Physical Damage is
affected by Physical Resistance Magical Damage Tooltip: Magical Damage is
affected by Magical Resistance Pure Damage Tooltip: Pure Damage is not
affected by any type of Resistance. Resistance Types Physical Resistance
Tooltip: Every point of Physical Resistance reduces Physical Damage taken
by 10%. Magical Resistance Tooltip: Every point of Magical Resistance
reduces Magical Damage taken by 10%. Units have Physical Damage, Magical

Damage or Magical Damage is the unit’s PRIMARY attack type, which dictates
the damage type of their basic attacks. Armour Types Light Moderate?

Gryphon Rider Population Building Dwarven Foundry Footman Archer Minotaur
Enforcer War Machine Nekroz Crypt Nekroz Defiler Archer Knight Vampyrium
Subjugator Nightsister Hideout Nightsister Acropolis scout unit Structures
& Building Structures are not built from workers, but instead from a
global building system accessible through the UI. This allows buildings to
be placed and built from anywhere inside friendly territory. Cancelling
the production of a building returns 75% of the invested resources. All
structures have an innate ability, Repair. Repair cannot be used if damage
has been taken by the structure in the past 5 seconds and will be
interrupted should any damage be taken. Repairing a structure restores any
lost health on it at the cost of resources. The cost and time taken to
repair is proportional to the structure’s cost and production time, and
proportional to the amount of health missing, being 25% of the structure’s
cost in resources and 100% of the structure’s production time to reach a
full repair from 1 health. This ability is self-cast by the structure and
does not require workers or any additional input. Upgrades Vision & Fog Of
War The map in standard matches, campaign missions and cooperative
assaults is partially revealed, being completely visible but obfuscated by
fog of war. Fog of war follows the traditional RTS functionality, being
revealed by units and their sight ranges, and displaying the last known
state of an area when obfuscated. Unit sight ranges can be blocked by
terrain, but units with Air Movement completely bypass this and have
unobstructed vision within their range. Terrain Being in a vertically
higher ground than another unit grants a vision advantage. Units in lower
ground have their vision obstructed to areas in higher ground, but units
in higher ground can completely see the area below. If a unit in higher
ground attacks or otherwise interacts with a unit in lower ground that
does not already have vision of it, it is individually revealed. Trees are
interactable and can be destroyed by certain units and abilities, allowing
for strategic formation of new pathways. Certain debris are also
interactable and can be destroyed by the player to open or close paths on
the terrain just the same. The terrain is implicitly divided as a grid,
which becomes visible to players when they attempt to place a structure.
This only affects the placement of structures and other terrain features
and is otherwise irrelevant for gameplay as units are not locked to this
grid. Scenario Initialization At the start of a standard multiplayer match
a faction hall is granted to each player at their starting location.
Alongside it, a scout unit is spawned, and a mine is created with 4
workers inside which are automatically ordered to gather at the mineral
cluster. Starting locations always contain a mineral cluster and an anima
strand within consistent distances from the faction hall. Standard matches
begin with players at 0 Minerals, 0 Anima, and a Population Cap of 10,
provided by their faction hall. As the player is granted a scout and four
workers, they effectively begin at 5/10 Population. Control Groups & Unit
Selection Rally to control group Control group setting - allow unit to be
part of multiple groups or not Formation movement Selecting multiple units
gives access to their abilities, provided they have different hotkeys?
Gameplay Modes Up to 8-player matches Capital loss win condition? Peace
timer in custom games Diplomacy game mode in which players can make
alliances Matchmaking glicko 2? Separate ratings for each race, solo and
team split ratings, random invisibly selects a race out of your ratings
and matches you if you were playing that; unranked mmr?; matching arranged
teams with soloq?think no Queue times; regions segments rating? Search
banding; percentile leagues; alongside mmr number; 5 placement games only;
4 seasons per year; multiple game mode queues; rating decay? Map vetos?
Omit revealing ratings & etc on loading screens/during match unless
players opt in; hide in post game as well? Locked ranked play until
requirement is met like X games in other modes? Artificial Intelligence AI
is utilized for non-player-controlled entities in the campaign and
cooperative assaults, and as bots for multiplayer modes. All modes
involving AI are divided into four difficulty settings: Novice, Adept,
Veteran, and Master. These difficulty settings dictate how skilful the AI
will be, and in some instances how much of the game it will have access
to. Multiplayer bots can have multiple build orders and strategies
programmed, which may be selected in the case of custom matches. Novice AI
Does not research Faction Weapon and Faction Armour upgrades. Is
handicapped in its ability to perform actions such as using unit
abilities. Has delayed attack timings and is very passive. Adept AI
Researches only the first level of Faction Weapon and Faction Armour
upgrades. Is handicapped in its ability to perform actions, but less so
than the Novice AI. Has delayed attack timings and is moderately passive.
Veteran AI Researches up to the second level of Faction Weapon and Faction
Armour upgrades. Is handicapped in its ability to perform actions, but
less so than the Adept AI. Does not have delayed attack timings and is
moderately aggressive. Master AI Researches up to the third level of
Faction Weapon and Faction Armour upgrades. Has no other sort of
handicaps, executing actions to the best of its capabilities. Does not
have delayed attack timings and is very aggressive. When playing alongside
AI teammates in multiplayer matches an additional interface menu will be
activated, allowing command over the AI such as choosing strategies for
opening builds and late game progression, requesting resources, commanding
attacks and defences, requesting scouting, requesting detection, and
suggesting expansion locations. mana shields on one of the factions like
protoss dragonhawk rider that can land like vikings global building with B
key, no workers needed. can only build in territory? building doesnt
deselect units begin match with workers on node and 0 resources building

of the screen. The game world cycles between

for timing or gating specific
contents. Daily Resets occur at 0:00 AM every day. Weekly Resets occur at

Innate Abilities are Abilities available by default on all
Champions in an Encounter.

Team Abilities are Abilities available to all Teams in an
Encounter. Any team member may utilize the Ability, placing it
under a shared cooldown.

Pawn Target abilities require vision over the target, whereas other
targeting types may still affect invisible pawns.,

All areas not within an allied pawn’s field of vision are hidden
by Fog of War. Enemy pawns are not visible in fog. Obstructed
vision is blocked by terrain

Invisible pawns are not revealed even when within allied field of
view, but have a distinctive refractive appearance that may be
seen with a keen eye.

Heartbringer is based on an established game genre, the
MOBA
. As with all that branches from an existing format, Heartbringer has many similarities and divergences to the common concept, and this section gives some explanations for these choices.

Many traditional mechanics in the genre were simply maintained from its roots as a mod for another game of a completely different genre, and therefore weren't ever designed specifically with the new genre's objectives in mind. Heartbringer aims to break free from these legacies and make choices that objectively work best for its intended experience.

“Last Hitting" is the concept of landing the killing blow on an enemy to gain an increased bounty of resources and experience (or often any bounty at all). Properly and continuously landing these last hits is typically crucial for the progression of a character during a match.

It's essentially a menial mechanical chore with no significant skill expression other than timing (which gets more and more lenient as the game progresses), yet with an immense and disproportionate impact in the game's results.
Dota 2

additionally possesses the mechanic of last hitting your own allies to deny the resources to the enemy, adding another layer of micromanagement (which loses relevance as the game progresses just as much).

This system introduces an unhealthy competition amongst teammates, which must either strategically agree on the funnelling of resources to a specific ally (and trusting said ally is capable of claiming the forfeited last hits) or fight amongst themselves to claim the last hit bounty, which creates the notion of "kill stealing".

This system introduces an unhealthy competition amongst teammates, which must either strategically agree on the funnelling of resources to a specific ally (and trusting said ally is capable of claiming the forfeited last hits) or fight amongst themselves to claim the last hit bounty, which creates the notion of "kill stealing".

The notion of "when" and "if" a last hit should be taken is extremely counterintuitive to the stated game objectives - defeat enemies and their structures - which makes the new player experience confusing, often leading to bad interactions with other players when new players don’t follow the optimal expected behaviour.

In addition to these issues, the bounty mechanic rewards typically only apply to players within a certain range of the defeated enemy (a team's NPC units do not count for this requirement). This means any enemies defeated away from a physical player presence do not contribute to the opposing team's progress, and makes it harder for players to see their allied NPC units as part of a team sharing the same objective.
Heroes of the Storm

removed resource bounties but their experience system remained tied to this same mechanic, leading to the concept of "Experience Soaking", a non-interactive gameplay responsibility disliked by many players.

Heartbringer gets rid of this mechanic entirely - loot bounties are automatically awarded to the opposing team regardless of proximity or who struck the killing blow (this includes NPC allied units). These bounty resources are additionally shared amongst the entire team, making it unnecessary to prioritize funnelling resources to any given teammate and allowing players to focus on the game’s core objectives instead.
Unlike
Dota 2
, Heartbringer makes no use of terrain elevation.
While Heartbringer employs Grand Tale's global synchronized time of day system, it doesn't have any gameplay impacts.

Heartbringer’s Team Ability system was specifically designed to encompass and adapt mechanics present in other games that weren’t properly part of a unified system, but many of these reference mechanics were actually discarded due to not aligning with our game’s objectives.

A “boot” was initially part of Heartbringer’s development in the form of “Wings” instead, but the concept was eventually dropped as the game’s design took shape and the mechanic was dropped as a whole.

The horn landed on the planet millenia ago, crashing into the side of a
mountain creating a huge crater. Its presence in the planet is what
explains the large concentration of phenomena happening specifically there
throughout the story. Trivia The idea of a horn being involved in the game
stems from the original Defense of the Ancients custom map for Warcraft 3,
which has the sound of a horn play at the start of a match. This same
element was preserved in the mod's sequel, Dota 2. The Arhus is partially
inspired by Heimdall in Norse mythology, who wielded the Gjallarhorn and
blew it to warn the gods of the start of Ragnarok, much like how the Arhus
is sounded at the start of a match. An interpretation of the name
"Gjallarhorn" is to mean "horn of the river Gjöll". In Norse mythology,
Gjöll is the river that separates the living from the dead, much like how
the Arhus has powers over life. Other than a musical instrument, horns
were historically also used as a drinking vessel, relating to . Heimdall
uses the Gjallarhorn to drink from the Mímisbrunnr to gain wisdom. Blowing
the Arhus is akin to drinking the waters of life. The name "Arhus" comes
from the river Aarhus in Jutland (Denmark), and roughly means "river
mouth". fountain of youth, water of life olifant/oliphant instrument The
Ohrn is a cosmogonic relic of insurmountable power which acts as the
tournament’s “artefact of attraction”. With its stalwart metal body and
elegant curves it guides the design of most of the game’s visual elements,
from architecture to interfaces. Its magic is divine in nature and
manifests as golden beams of energy, representing the vital energy that
flows through every living being. Sound can be heard across the universe.
Inscription ``` Flavour Text "While you live, sound - have all grief be
through, for the end exists avowed and such demands its due." -
Inscription on the Ohrn Also known as the Cradle ``` reduced for is a
necklace ``` The Ohrn's name is based on the Kabbalistic concept of the
Ohr
, as well as a pun on the misspelling of "horn".
Shofar
The inscription on the Ohrn is inspired by the
One Ring
, and the inscription itself was written based on the
Seikilos Epitaph
, considered to be the oldest complete musical composition every found and from which the Grand Tale Theme motif is also derived from.

Cards are the main playing piece in Tale C.
Cards are all canonically designed and drawn by Leah Lightbringer, and represent the many personalities and events the Grand Tale comes across.
Cards share many properties, with some being specific to a given Card Type.
A Card may have from one to two Alignments, and may only be placed in a deck alongside a Hero which contains it's alignment.
A Card's Type determines how it's used in gameplay. A Card may only be of a single type, out of the following:
The Board is the playable zone in a Tale C match, and where the various Card Zones are located.
The board is divided into various Zones, in which specific card types are placed.
